steps.title: Keep one main verb
steps.content: The sentence's main verb is "might instead procure." The words between "Antiquarians" and that main verb must describe which antiquarians, not create another finite verb.

steps.title: Reject finite forms
steps.content: A, B, and D are finite verb forms, so each would create an unconnected clause before "might instead procure." A participle can modify "Antiquarians" and keep the sentence grammatical. The answer is C.
